name: MiniMod Transistor Ladder Filter
description: Faithful 24dB/oct transistor ladder LPF replica of the early Model D filter. Matched discrete ladder transistors, three-input audio mixer, vactrol VC resonance, Model D 1/3 and 2/3 keyboard tracking CVs, internal overdrive jumper.

controls.name: FREQ
controls.description: Large cutoff frequency knob. Acts as offset when CV is applied. CCW fully closes; CW fully opens.
controls.detail: Full audio range · offset when CV present

controls.name: FREQ CV
controls.description: Attenuator for the Freq CV Input. Ranges 0-130%, enabling overmodulation past 1V/oct tracking. 1V/oct point occurs at 2 o'clock (position 7).

controls.name: EMP CV
controls.description: Attenuator for the Emphasis CV input feeding the vactrol resonance circuit. Manual Emphasis still acts as offset when CV is present.

controls.name: EMPHASIS
controls.description: Manual resonance (feedback) control. Self-oscillates between positions 8 and 10 producing a playable sine wave.
controls.detail: 0–10 · self-oscillation ~8+ · 3–4 octave tracking range in self-osc

controls.name: INPUT 1 / INPUT 2 / INPUT 3
controls.description: Three independent level knobs forming an input mixer into the filter core. All AC-coupled.

controls.name: Gain Jumper (J1)
controls.description: Removing the onboard jumper boosts pre-filter gain by ~70% for Model D style overdrive. Output level rises proportionally.

inputs.name: IN 1
inputs.description: Audio input 1 into the three-channel mixer. AC-coupled.

inputs.name: IN 2
inputs.description: Audio input 2 into the three-channel mixer. AC-coupled.

inputs.name: IN 3
inputs.description: Audio input 3 into the three-channel mixer. AC-coupled.

inputs.name: FREQ-CV
inputs.description: External cutoff CV. Scaled by FREQ CV attenuator. Overmodulation possible past 100%.

inputs.name: 1V/Oct
inputs.description: Fixed 1V/octave input. Emulates Model D keyboard switches 1 AND 2 on. Tracking drifts with temperature (uncorrected, per original).

inputs.name: 1/3 CV
inputs.description: Scaled keyboard tracking input at 3V per octave. Equivalent to Model D keyboard switch 1 on, switch 2 off.

inputs.name: 2/3 CV
inputs.description: Scaled keyboard tracking input at 1.5V per octave. Equivalent to Model D keyboard switch 2 on, switch 1 off.

inputs.name: EMP-CV
inputs.description: Resonance/emphasis CV input. Fed through vactrol for smooth response up into audio rate.

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: Filtered audio output after the ladder core. Unity gain with single input at 100%; +70% higher with gain jumper removed.

behaviors.name: Three keyboard-tracking CV inputs
behaviors.trigger: applying V/oct to 1V/Oct, 1/3 CV, or 2/3 CV
behaviors.description: Recreates the Model D filter-keyboard-follow switches. 1V/Oct = both switches on (full tracking), 1/3 CV = 3V/oct (partial), 2/3 CV = 1.5V/oct (two-thirds). Summing them produces classic Moog filter tracking behavior without a dedicated switch.

behaviors.name: Vactrol voltage-controlled resonance
behaviors.description: Resonance is controlled by a fast vactrol, acting purely resistively so the filter core sound is unchanged. Supports audio-rate modulation for FM-like resonance timbres. Manual Emphasis becomes an offset when CV is present.

behaviors.name: Overmodulation via Freq CV
behaviors.description: Freq CV range extends to 130% versus the 1V/Oct input. Pushing past 1V/oct point creates overshoot and exaggerated tracking useful for filter FM timbres and wide modulation sweeps.

behaviors.name: Self-oscillation as sine VCO
behaviors.trigger: EMPHASIS set 8–10 with no audio input
behaviors.description: Filter self-resonates into a clean sine wave. Tracks V/Oct reasonably over 3–4 octaves (CV scaling is uncorrected, drifts slightly with ambient temperature, matching original Model D behavior). Playable as an additional VCO.

behaviors.name: Internal overdrive jumper
behaviors.description: Boosts pre-filter signal gain by approximately 70%, overdriving the ladder front-end. Emulates the vintage Model D trick of routing the headphone output back into the external VCF input. Output level rises proportionally, so compensate downstream.

behaviors.name: Emphasis still active with CV
behaviors.trigger: patching EMP-CV while Emphasis knob set
behaviors.description: Manual Emphasis knob is summed as offset under CV control. Allows biasing the resonance up to near-oscillation then modulating the peak region without going fully into self-oscillation.

patchIdeas.name: Model D bass tracking
patchIdeas.patch: Sequencer V/oct → 1V/Oct filter input · VCO into IN 1 · Envelope into FREQ-CV with FREQ CV at 2 o'clock · EMPHASIS ~5 · classic Moog-style filter envelope with full keyboard tracking
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCF:
* EMPHASIS: 5
* FREQ CV: 2 o'clock (pos 7)

VCO:

Sequencer:

Envelope:

VCA:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> VCF (1V/Oct)
- VCO (Saw) -> VCF (IN 1)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCF (FREQ-CV)
- VCF (OUT) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Two-thirds tracking lead
patchIdeas.patch: Pitch CV into 2/3 CV only · filter tracks at 1.5V/oct for that slightly-detuned 'half-follow' Model D character · EMPHASIS ~7 just below self-osc · bright but never fully opens up the top
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCF:
* EMPHASIS: 7
* FREQ knob: noon

VCO:

Sequencer:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Sequencer (Pitch) >> VCF (2/3 CV) // 1.5V/oct tracking
- VCO (Saw) -> VCF (IN 1)
- VCF (OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Three-oscillator fat unison
patchIdeas.patch: Three detuned VCOs into IN 1-3 · levels balanced ~70% each · J1 jumper removed for pre-filter overdrive · EMPHASIS ~4 keeps the fatness without squealing · classic thick Moog bass
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCF:
* J1 jumper: REMOVED (overdrive)
* INPUT 1-3: ~70%
* EMPHASIS: 4

VCO 1:

VCO 2:

VCO 3:

Envelope:

VCA:

- VCO 1 (Saw) -> VCF (IN 1)
- VCO 2 (Saw) -> VCF (IN 2)
- VCO 3 (Pulse) -> VCF (IN 3)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCF (FREQ-CV)
- VCF (OUT) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Sine VCO from self-oscillation
patchIdeas.patch: No audio input · EMPHASIS at 10 · 1V/Oct from sequencer · filter sings as a sinewave VCO tracking ~3-4 octaves · FM the cutoff via FREQ-CV for timbral motion
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCF:
* EMPHASIS: 10 (self-osc)
* INPUT 1-3: 0

Sequencer:

LFO:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> VCF (1V/Oct)
- LFO (Out) >> VCF (FREQ-CV) // timbral FM
- VCF (OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Audio-rate resonance FM
patchIdeas.patch: VCO sine into EMP-CV · EMP CV knob ~5 · filter IN 1 fed by a second VCO · EMPHASIS 6 · vactrol modulates resonance at audio rate producing FM-like sidebands unique to this filter
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCF:
* EMPHASIS: 6
* EMP CV: 5

VCO A:

VCO B:

VCA:

- VCO A (Saw) -> VCF (IN 1)
- VCO B (Triangle) >> VCF (EMP-CV) // audio-rate resonance mod
- VCF (OUT) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Dirty Model-D trick
patchIdeas.patch: J1 removed · EMPHASIS ~8 (just oscillating) · feed audio into IN 1 at maximum · VCA after filter attenuates the boosted output · saturated resonance growl like the vintage headphone-feedback trick
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCF:
* J1 jumper: REMOVED
* INPUT 1: 10 (max)
* EMPHASIS: 8

VCO:

Envelope:

VCA:

- VCO (Saw) -> VCF (IN 1)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCF (FREQ-CV)
- VCF (OUT) -> VCA (In) // VCA turned down to compensate
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Overmodulation sweep
patchIdeas.patch: FREQ CV knob at maximum (130%) · wide envelope into FREQ-CV · filter cutoff is driven past its normal limits giving an exaggerated, dramatic sweep with pronounced pop on envelope peaks
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCF:
* FREQ CV: max (130%)
* EMPHASIS: 6

VCO:

Envelope:

- VCO (Saw) -> VCF (IN 1)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCF (FREQ-CV) // overmodulated sweep
- VCF (OUT) -> Output (In)
